blockingIssues.description: `lib/exhaust.ml` infers `ExprIf` enum type information from only the then-branch final expression, so a let-bound value can be cached under the wrong enum when the else branch has a different effective type.

`lib/exhaust.ml` infers `ExprMatch` enum type information from only the first arm expression, which can poison the exhaustiveness type environment with a non-authoritative enum name and produce bogus missing-variant diagnostics later.
